text.title: Agent Pricing & Unit Economics Decision Tree

decision-tree.root-question: Are per-task COGS measured (not estimated) AND does the proposed price hit the gross-margin floor?

branch.question: What is the COGS variance class of the workload?

branch.conclusion: Per-seat pricing with a fair-use MAU cap. Apply r4-cap-protect-heavy-users to set the cap.
Metered (per-task) pricing with credit-rollover or burst allowance. Apply r4-cap-protect-heavy-users.
Outcome-based pricing (price per resolved ticket, per converted lead, etc.) — flat seat or per-task models will either leak margin or scare risk-averse buyers.
Block. Measure 100+ real tasks before drafting pricing — estimates from one-shot demos underprice 3-10×.
Block. Reduce COGS (cheaper model, retrieval cache, output truncation) OR raise price OR change pricing model — shipping below 60% margin is unrecoverable post-launch.

example.p: Measured COGS = $0.18/task, target price = $0.50/task → 64% margin. Variance is mid (heavy-user p95 = 3× median). → conclusion r3-pricing-model-fits-variance "metered" → 1000 tasks/month credit, $0.05 overage, fair-use cap at 5000/month.
